rationale: No existing spec exercises the new QueryErrorState component's interaction surface: error-code-specific action cards (fix-query, configure-resource, expand-range), the collapsible ErrorDetailPanel (expand/collapse toggle), the copy-error button, filter validation errors, or the new data-test selectors (query-error-fix-query-card, error-detail-toggle-btn, query-error-copy-btn, etc.). Existing error tests in logsqueries.spec.js, logshistogram.spec.js, and logsquickmode.spec.js only assert that an error message appears (expectErrorMessageVisible/clickErrorMessage) — they do not verify the structural component rendering, per-code action cards, or user interactions with the new component. The feature (new universal QueryErrorState component with hero/block layouts) is substantial enough to warrant its own dedicated spec. IncidentList.vue status filter changes are explicitly out of scope per the feature design doc (Edge Case 8).
